steps.title: Write the count equation
steps.content:
The variables $b$ and $g$ count the numbers of costumes. Since the teacher ordered $30$ costumes total, the count equation is:
$$b+g=30$$

steps.title: Write the cost equation
steps.content:
Each boys' costume costs $24$ dollars, and each girls' costume costs $33$ dollars. The total cost is $837$ dollars, so:
$$24b+33g=837$$

steps.title: Match both equations
steps.content:
The correct system must pair the total-count equation with the total-cost equation:
$$24b+33g=837\quad \text{and}\quad b+g=30$$The answer is B.
